page_or_feature: Admin section pages have no per-section guard
issue_description: No admin page calls admin_can(section); the only gate is the layout's is-any-admin check. A sub-admin granted one section can open any other admin route by URL and the page loads and fetches data. admin_can() is never invoked from app code, only from RLS.
business_impact: Insider data leak across admin sections; weakens least-privilege promise to future admin hires
user_impact: Sub-admin sees data for sections they were never granted
technical_impact: Read-path relies entirely on table-level RLS which is not uniformly admin_can-gated
recommended_fix: Add an explicit admin_can(section) check at the top of every admin/*/page.tsx and redirect if false

page_or_feature: Business OS tables enforce can_manage_store not staff_can
issue_description: Sidebar hides customers/inventory/pos/suppliers/tasks from staff without the mapped permission, but RLS on those tables (0068/0069/0070) uses can_manage_store (any staff) instead of staff_can(perm). A bookings-only staffer can read/write CRM, inventory, POS via direct URL or API.
business_impact: Owner believes staff access is scoped when it is not; data exposure to junior staff
user_impact: Staff reach modules the owner intended to hide
technical_impact: Nav gate is cosmetic for OS modules; real boundary is only 3 perms orders/products/bookings
recommended_fix: Introduce granular perm keys for OS modules and re-point RLS to staff_can, or document that all staff have full OS access

page_or_feature: Admin write RLS not uniformly migrated to admin_can
issue_description: Only jobs/freelance/wholesale/messages/questions/growth/deals/pages/audit were re-pointed to admin_can(section). stores/orders/reviews/verifications/subscriptions/delivery/academy/leaders/business_types/taxonomy/settings/users write policies live elsewhere (likely is_super_admin), so a sub-admin's write path is inconsistent.
business_impact: Unpredictable sub-admin capability; some sections silently super-only
user_impact: Sub-admin actions may fail or succeed unexpectedly
recommended_fix: Audit every admin-managed table and standardize write policies on admin_can(section)

page_or_feature: Dashboard bloat: ~22 sidebar items on day one
issue_description: A new store faces ~20-22 sidebar modules, ~13 Pro-locked, nearly all pointing at zero-data pages. No Lite mode, no progressive disclosure. Target user is a low-tech small business.
business_impact: Highest churn risk for the core persona; overwhelmed merchants abandon after first session
technical_impact: Every sector lists all modules; no simplified default
recommended_fix: Add a Lite/starter mode: OS home leads with 3 actions (add product, share store, messages), collapse advanced modules behind a toggle

page_or_feature: Retention tools (CRM/reports/POS) are all Pro-locked
issue_description: Customers, reports, POS, tasks, inventory, automations, campaigns, kitchen, suppliers, accounting, coupons, doctors, tools are all pro:true. A free merchant with 0 orders sees ProGate upsell instead of an empty CRM, blocking the very tools that prove value.
business_impact: Blocks activation of free merchants; upsell-before-value hurts conversion
user_impact: Free merchant cannot experience retention value
recommended_fix: Give limited free access (read-only CRM, basic reports) to demonstrate value before paywall

page_or_feature: Verification approve AND reject are one-click with no confirm
issue_description: Both approve and reject of a submitted store credential fire immediately with no window.confirm. Rejecting a legal credential is consequential and irreversible in UX terms.
business_impact: Accidental rejection of merchant credentials damages trust and support load
user_impact: Admin can misclick and reject a verification
technical_impact: No confirmation dialog on a consequential action
recommended_fix: Add confirm() to reject (and ideally approve) in the verification actions component

page_or_feature: Subscription activate has no confirmation
issue_description: Manual Pro activation inserts a payment row and flips the store to pro/verified with a single click and no confirmation.
business_impact: Accidental Pro grants distort billing records and revenue reporting
user_impact: Admin can misclick and grant paid plan
recommended_fix: Add confirmation dialog summarizing store, period, and price before activating

page_or_feature: Granting admin permissions has no confirm dialog
issue_description: savePerms grants a user access to admin sections (privilege-escalation-class action) with one click, no confirmation. DB trigger enforces super-only, but the UX has no safeguard.
business_impact: Accidental admin grants are a security-governance risk
user_impact: Admin can misclick and elevate a user
recommended_fix: Add a confirmation dialog listing the sections being granted/revoked

page_or_feature: Mass push broadcast is not audit-logged
issue_description: AdminBroadcast sends Web Push to all subscribers via /api/push/broadcast. It is confirmed but never calls logAdminAction, so a mass-notification action leaves no audit trail.
business_impact: Untracked mass-communication; no accountability for spam or mistakes
user_impact: Users receive broadcasts with no admin trail
recommended_fix: Call logAdminAction('created','message',null,{broadcast:true}) after a successful broadcast

page_or_feature: endClearance lacks confirm while endFlash has one
issue_description: Deals page: endFlash is confirmed (line 54) but endClearance is one-click (line 69). Inconsistent guarding of equivalent destructive actions.
business_impact: Accidental end of a clearance campaign
recommended_fix: Add confirm() to endClearance to match endFlash

page_or_feature: Audit logging is fire-and-forget and swallows all errors
issue_description: logAdminAction is a client-side RPC that catches and discards every error. A failed audit write is invisible, and coverage depends on developers remembering to call it after each mutation.
business_impact: Compliance/forensics gaps with no signal that the trail is incomplete
technical_impact: No guarantee every sensitive change is logged
recommended_fix: Move critical audit logging to DB triggers on sensitive tables so it cannot be skipped or silently fail

page_or_feature: No bulk actions on any admin list
issue_description: No admin page supports multi-select or bulk approve/reject/delete. Every mutation is per-row. Painful as moderation queues grow.
business_impact: Admin throughput bottleneck at scale
technical_impact: Missing feature across all list clients
recommended_fix: Add multi-select + bulk operations to moderation lists (market, jobs, freelance, wholesale, reviews, questions)

page_or_feature: No pagination anywhere in admin; fixed limits only
issue_description: admin/stores loads all non-deleted stores with no limit; other pages rely on fixed .limit() caps (100-5000) with no UI to page beyond. Fine at 14 stores, breaks at scale.
business_impact: Slow admin pages and truncated data as the platform grows
user_impact: Admin cannot see beyond the cap
recommended_fix: Add server-side pagination to admin list pages

page_or_feature: Search missing on high-growth admin queues
issue_description: No search box on orders, taxonomy, market/reports, delivery, leaders, academy, reviews, verifications, deals, business-types, pages, audit. Leaders/academy/verifications/audit queues will grow unsearchable.
business_impact: Admins cannot find records in growing queues
recommended_fix: Add search inputs to the listed admin pages, prioritizing verifications/audit/leaders/academy

page_or_feature: All admin deletes are hard deletes
issue_description: Listings, jobs/gigs/wholesale, reviews, messages, questions, leaders, academy, business_types, taxonomy, delivery, site_pages are hard-deleted via .delete(), guarded only by native window.confirm. Only stores use soft-delete.
business_impact: Irreversible data loss; broken referential integrity for related records
recommended_fix: Adopt soft-delete (deleted_at) for entities with relationships, matching the stores pattern

page_or_feature: Order empty-state has no next-step CTA
issue_description: With 0 orders the page shows a bare 'No orders for this store yet' dashed box with no guidance (share your link, add products, make a POS sale). The real DB has 0 orders so every merchant sees this.
business_impact: Missed activation nudge at the exact moment a merchant needs direction
user_impact: Merchant left without a next step
recommended_fix: Add action buttons to the empty state: share store link, add product, record a POS sale

page_or_feature: Reports empty-state is sterile at zero
issue_description: With 0 orders, KPIs show 0/$0, revenue columns render as flat stubs and each Bars panel shows noData. Functional but discouraging with no guidance.
business_impact: Weak first impression of analytics value
recommended_fix: Add an explanatory zero-state with a link to add products or share the store

page_or_feature: Product list at /items but edit at /products/[id]; products/page.tsx absent
issue_description: The catalog list lives at /items while single-product edit is at /products/[productId]; there is no /products list page. Confusing navigation model.
business_impact: Developer and support confusion; inconsistent mental model
user_impact: Merchant/URL confusion between list and edit
recommended_fix: Unify under one segment (e.g. /products for list + /products/[id] for edit) or alias /items

page_or_feature: OS home issues 16 parallel queries per load
issue_description: The sector-adaptive home fires ~16 parallel queries (store_report RPC plus head-counts and lists) on every load. Harmless at demo scale, costly at volume.
business_impact: Higher DB load and latency as usage grows
recommended_fix: Consolidate counts into a single RPC and cache where possible

page_or_feature: No clear cash-on-delivery / no-payment messaging on product
issue_description: There is no explicit 'pay on delivery, no card needed' line on the product/store page. The target Lebanese user distrusts online payment; the platform's biggest trust lever is unstated.
business_impact: Lost conversions from trust-hesitant buyers
recommended_fix: Add a persistent 'Pay on delivery - no card needed' line on product and store pages

page_or_feature: merchant/new does not lead with free/no-card/WhatsApp value
issue_description: The store-creation entry does not clearly state 'free, no card, pay on delivery, share on WhatsApp' - the exact reassurances the WhatsApp-heavy low-tech target needs.
business_impact: Weak activation of the core acquisition persona
technical_impact: Value proposition not surfaced at entry
recommended_fix: Add a concise value banner to merchant/new in plain Lebanese Arabic

page_or_feature: Staff manager does not explain the 3-permission scope
issue_description: The staff UI exposes orders/products/bookings toggles but does not tell the owner that all other OS tools (CRM, accounting, POS) are available to any staff member regardless of these toggles.
business_impact: Owner mis-assumes granular isolation and grants staff too much
recommended_fix: Add helper text clarifying that the three toggles gate orders/products/bookings and other tools are open to all staff

page_or_feature: Multi-branch shares inventory with no per-branch stock
issue_description: Branches (0084/0085) share a single stock pool; no per-branch inventory, hours, or plan. A real multi-location business with separate warehouses will hit this wall, and the UI does not disclose it.
business_impact: Blocks enterprise/multi-branch adoption; surprise limitation
recommended_fix: Disclose 'stock is shared across branches' in the branches module; plan per-branch stock as a roadmap item

page_or_feature: admin-leader-actions uses hardcoded Arabic strings
issue_description: Confirm and error strings are hardcoded Arabic instead of the dictionary, breaking i18n and the English admin experience.
business_impact: Inconsistent localization in admin
recommended_fix: Move strings to dict.admin and reference them

page_or_feature: No legal entity or merchant terms surfaced
issue_description: Privacy exists at /privacy but there is no visible company/legal-entity info or merchant terms of service, and no public consumer-complaint channel (reports are admin-only).
business_impact: Weakens legitimacy for regulators and cautious users
user_impact: User cannot verify who operates the platform
recommended_fix: Add legal entity details and merchant ToS to the footer; expose a public complaint path

page_or_feature: Public surfaces expose empty catalog with no traction framing
issue_description: With 14 stores, 24 products and 0 orders, explore/product pages look empty. No content or narrative fills the gap (the no-fabricated-data rule is correct, but leaves surfaces bare).
business_impact: Poor first impression for investors and early users
recommended_fix: Lead public pages with the Business OS wedge and evergreen content (guides/hub) rather than a thin catalog

page_or_feature: Pro-locked modules advertise the roadmap to competitors
issue_description: A free store's sidebar renders ~13 Pro-locked modules by name, disclosing planned/premium capabilities to anyone who signs up.
business_impact: Free reconnaissance for competitors
recommended_fix: Hide modules the store's plan/sector will never use rather than showing locks for everything

page_or_feature: Sidebar switches at lg leaving a tablet gap
issue_description: The merchant sidebar is lg:flex-row (drawer below lg). Tablet (768-1024) falls between the mobile drawer and desktop rail, risking an awkward layout, while header controls appear at md.
business_impact: Degraded tablet experience for a common device
user_impact: Tablet users get an in-between layout
technical_impact: Breakpoint mismatch (md header vs lg sidebar)
recommended_fix: Test and tune tablet breakpoints; align header and sidebar thresholds

page_or_feature: 25-item sidebar becomes a long drawer on mobile
issue_description: On mobile the full module list renders in an RTL drawer, producing a long scroll that amplifies the dashboard-bloat problem on the smallest screen.
business_impact: Compounds churn risk on mobile-first target
user_impact: Long scroll to reach a module
recommended_fix: Apply the Lite mode / grouping on mobile so the drawer stays short

page_or_feature: Staff delete reuses products.confirmDelete copy
issue_description: The staff removal confirm dialog reuses products.confirmDelete text, so the message does not match the action.
business_impact: Minor confusion in a destructive dialog
recommended_fix: Add a dedicated staff.confirmRemove dictionary key

page_or_feature: Unmapped business-type slug silently filed under shopping
issue_description: The business-type to group mapping uses a silent '?? shopping' fallback, so an unmapped sector slug is quietly categorized as Shopping instead of surfacing an error.
business_impact: Miscategorized stores skew discovery and analytics
user_impact: Store lands in the wrong top-level group
recommended_fix: Log or surface unmapped slugs instead of defaulting silently

page_or_feature: Exchange-rate save has no confirmation
issue_description: Saving the USD to LBP rate is one-click with no confirm, yet it affects pricing across the entire platform.
business_impact: A wrong rate mispricing every product platform-wide
technical_impact: No guard on a global-impact setting
recommended_fix: Add a confirmation showing old vs new rate before saving

page_or_feature: No verified check that PII stays out of URLs
issue_description: Search and listing params should be verified to never carry personal data in query strings; not confirmed by reading.
business_impact: Potential PII leakage in logs/referrers
recommended_fix: Audit query params on search/orders/track to ensure no PII is placed in URLs

page_or_feature: Onboarding guidance exists only on OS home, absent on sub-pages
issue_description: The strong checklist and rule-based suggestions live only on the OS home. When a merchant clicks a sidebar module they land on a bare empty state with no next step.
business_impact: Drop-off after leaving the home page
recommended_fix: Add contextual next-step hints to key sub-page empty states (orders, customers, reports)
